steps.title: Track the comparison
steps.content: The underlined portion describes what most similar manuscripts contained: celebrated medieval authors and readings favored by elites. The word "whereas" then contrasts that norm with The Heege Manuscript, which emphasizes popular entertainments and practical advice.

steps.title: Discard unsupported purposes
steps.content: Choice B introduces how common hand-copied collections were, which the text does not discuss. Choice C says the Findern Anthology is a poor comparison, but it is used as a useful contrast. Choice D reverses the focus by making the passage about understanding the Findern Anthology.

steps.title: State the function
steps.content: The underlined portion gives context for why The Heege Manuscript stands out from other collections of its time. The answer is A.
